opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My chest is locked tight and I can't catch my breath; I am so exhausted from constantly defending my right to just exist in public.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on suddenly this evening after a frightening confrontation with strangers in public — my chest went tight, I couldn't stop breathing fast, and my heart is pounding, and it won't settle.

symptom_narrative.onset: suddenly, this evening, a few minutes after a hostile confrontation with strangers in a public place while I was out with my partner
symptom_narrative.duration: about an hour and still going
symptom_narrative.character: my chest feels locked and tight, like a band clamped around it; my breathing is fast and shallow and I can't seem to get a full breath in; my heart is racing and thumping
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: really frightening — the worst it has ever felt physically; I can't seem to calm myself down
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: across the middle and front of my chest, and my neck and shoulders feel clenched up
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, coming in waves
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much; my partner is trying to breathe slowly with me but it isn't really working
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: thinking about what happened, and when I try to breathe deliberately it seems to make the panic worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: shaky hands, a bit lightheaded, drained and exhausted, and very on-edge and tearful
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get keyed-up and physically tense a lot, especially around new people or being out in public, but it has never hit me this hard in my body before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't leave the house right now and I can barely get a sentence out without gasping

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: no ongoing medical conditions that I know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I'm queer and I feel like I'm always having to come out and correct people's assumptions — on medical forms with no inclusive language, with every new provider, and just out in public. It's draining. Tonight someone confronted us for showing affection and it really shook me. Lately I've been wanting to avoid going out, and honestly avoid appointments too, because I'm so tired of it.

communication_style.typical_message_style: guarded at first, softening once they sense the clinician is safe and non-judgmental

reluctance_topics: explaining the details of the confrontation and their identity until they feel the clinical space is safe and affirming

objective_data_offered.value: breathing fast and shallow, visibly gasping between words
partner reports they look pale and a bit sweaty, are pacing, and keep rubbing the middle of their chest
fine trembling of the hands visible on video
my heart feels like it's racing and pounding, faster than I've ever felt it
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: a felt sensation, not a measured pulse — no home device used
